zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Scales於1335年4月2日之後出生在英國英格蘭賀特福爾時，她的父親Robert de Scales 3rd Baron Scales8687歲，母親Catherine de Ufford8681歲。她於1363年3月10日之前與Sir Robert Howard在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1416年5月8日在英國諾福爾可易亞斯特 文其，埋葬在英國諾福爾可易亞斯特 文其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Scales于1335年4月2日之后出生在英国英格兰賀特福爾时，她的父亲Robert de Scales 3rd Baron Scales8687岁，母亲Catherine de Ufford8681岁。她于1363年3月10日之前与Sir Robert Howard在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1416年5月8日在英国諾福爾可易亞斯特 文其，埋葬在英国諾福爾可易亞斯特 文其。

nameMeanings.name: Scales
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: plural form of   Scale  , naming a herdsman living and working on a summer pasture, or a habitational name for someone from a place called Scales, mostly minor places in Lancashire and Yorkshire.   Scholes   is a dialect variant of the same name.

(of Norman origin): habitational name either from Écalles-Alix (Seine-Maritime), Écalles (in Estouteville-Écalles, Seine-Maritime), or from Escalles (Pas-de-Calais). The earliest spellings of these names suggest a derivation from Latin scala, scalae (plural) ‘ladder, staircase, stile’, perhaps applied to a steep slope or path, although Old Norse skáli (‘herdsman's hut, shieling’, see Scale ) has also been suggested for Écalles. Challis may be a variant of the same name. The surname has also been established in southern Ireland (Limerick) since the 14th century.

variants.name: Scholes
meanings: English: habitational name from one or other of the places called Scholes in Lancashire and Yorkshire, or from Scole in Norfolk, all named with Middle English scole or scoles ‘herdsman's hut(s)’ (Old Norse skáli). In northern England the name was sometimes interchangeable with Scales , a dialect variant of the same word.

variants.name: Scale
meanings: English: from northern Middle English scale ‘herdsman's hut, shed, shieling’ (Old Norse skáli). The surname may have denoted a herdsman who lived and worked on a summer pasture or someone from any of several mostly minor places so named in Lancashire and Yorkshire. The name often varies between singular and plural forms; see Scales .

variants.name: Schooley
meanings: English (Sussex): variant of Scholey, a habitational name either from Scholey's Bridge in Hemsworth (Yorkshire) or the lost Scholey in Rastrick (Yorkshire), recorded in that spelling in 1399. The placenames derive from Old Norse skáli ‘(temporary) hut, shed, shieling’ + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’. Compare Scales .

variants.name: Schoolcraft
meanings: English: variant of Scowcroft, from Middle English scale, sc(h)ole ‘hut, shed’ (Old Norse skáli) + croft ‘small enclosed field’. The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a field containing a hut, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Scowcroft in Oldham (Lancashire) and Scholecroft in Morley (Yorkshire). Compare Scales and Schofield .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Scales was born after 2 April 1335, in Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Robert de Scales 3rd Baron Scales, was 8687 and her mother, Catherine de Ufford, was 8681. She married Sir Robert Howard before 10 March 1363, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 8 May 1416, in East Winch, Norfolk, England, and was buried in East Winch, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Scales est née après 2 avril 1335, à Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert de Scales 3rd Baron Scales, avait 8687 ans et sa mère, Catherine de Ufford, avait 8681 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Robert Howard avant 10 mars 1363, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 8 mai 1416, à East Winch, Norfolk, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à East Winch, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Scales nach 2. April 1335 in Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert de Scales 3rd Baron Scales 8687 und ihre Mutter Catherine de Ufford 8681. Sie hat vor 10. März 1363 in England, United Kingdom Sir Robert Howard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 8. Mai 1416 in East Winch, Norfolk, England und wurde in East Winch, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Scales è nata il dopo il 2 aprile 1335 nel Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Robert de Scales 3rd Baron Scales, aveva 8687 anni e sua madre, Catherine de Ufford, aveva 8681 anni. Lei sposato Sir Robert Howard prima del 10 marzo 1363, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 8 maggio 1416, nel East Winch, Norfolk, England, e fu sepolta a East Winch, Norfolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Scalesが1335年4月2日以後にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父Robert de Scales 3rd Baron Scalesは8687歳，母Catherine de Uffordは8681歳であった。1363年3月10日以前にイギリスイギリスでSir Robert Howardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1416年5月8日にイギリスノーフォルクエアスト ウィンチで死去し，イギリスノーフォルクエアスト ウィンチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Scales(이)가 1335년 4월 2일 이후 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Scales 3rd Baron Scales(은)는 8687세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Catherine de Ufford(은)는 8681세였다. 그녀는 1363년 3월 10일 이전 영국 잉글랜드에서 Sir Robert Howard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1416년 5월 8일 영국 노르폴크 이스트 윈치에서 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 이스트 윈치에 묻혔다.

pt.Aft 2 April 1335–8 May 1416: após 2 de abril de 1335–8 de maio de 1416
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Scales nasceu após 2 de abril de 1335, em Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Robert de Scales 3rd Baron Scales, tinha 8687 anos e sua mãe, Catherine de Ufford, tinha 8681 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Robert Howard antes 10 de março de 1363, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 8 de maio de 1416, em East Winch, Norfolk, Inglaterra, e foi sepultada em East Winch, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Scales родилась после 2 апрель 1335 г. в местности хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Robert de Scales 3rd Baron Scales было 8687 лет, а матери, которую звали Catherine de Ufford, 8681. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Robert Howard перед 10 марта 1363 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 8 мая 1416 г. в местности эаст винч, норфолк, Англия и была похоронена в местности эаст винч, норфолк, Англия.

Se casó con Sir Robert Howard antes de 10 de marzo de 1363, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 8 de mayo de 1416, en East Winch, Norfolk, Inglaterra, y fue sepultada en East Winch, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
